     1	// CryptoTests.cs — xunit tests for src/Crypto.cs.
     2	//
     3	// Vector sources:
     4	//   - HKDF RFC 5869 test cases A.1..A.3 (the SHA-256 cases)
     5	//   - NIST P-256 known points (G and 2G) for GetPublicKey
     6	//   - Compress/UncompressRawPublicKey roundtrip
     7	//   - HpkeEncrypt/HpkeDecrypt roundtrip (deterministic round-trip)
     8	//   - VerifySessionJwtSignature negative cases
     9	//
    10	// Tests that require a Turnkey-signed bundle (DecryptCredentialBundle,
    11	// EncryptPrivateKeyToBundle, DecryptExportBundle against PRODUCTION_SIGNER)
    12	// are not unit-testable without a real Turnkey signing key. They are
    13	// implicitly exercised by the HPKE roundtrip (the inner HPKE step is
    14	// identical) and by the E2E whoami flow once credentials are present.
    15	
    16	using System;
    17	using System.Text;
    18	using FluentAssertions;
    19	using Org.BouncyCastle.Math;
    20	using Xunit;
    21	
    22	namespace Turnkey.Tests
    23	{
    24	    public class CryptoTests
    25	    {
    26	        // ============================================================
    27	        // Math.ModSqrt — Tonelli-Shanks
    28	        // ============================================================
    29	
    30	        [Fact]
    31	        public void ModSqrt_SmallPrime_HappyPath()
    32	        {
    33	            // 4 = 2^2 mod 7, ModSqrt(4, 7) should be 2 or 5 (-2 mod 7).
    34	            var p = new BigInteger("7");
    35	            var x = new BigInteger("4");
    36	            var root = Crypto.Math.ModSqrt(x, p);
    37	            (root.Equals(new BigInteger("2")) || root.Equals(new BigInteger("5")))
    38	                .Should().BeTrue($"got {root}");
    39	        }
    40	
    41	        [Fact]
    42	        public void ModSqrt_NonResidue_Throws()
    43	        {
    44	            // 3 is a non-residue mod 7.
    45	            var p = new BigInteger("7");
    46	            var x = new BigInteger("3");
    47	            Action act = () => Crypto.Math.ModSqrt(x, p);
    48	            act.Should().Throw<InvalidOperationException>()
    49	               .WithMessage("could not find a modular square root");
    50	        }
    51	
    52	        [Fact]
    53	        public void ModSqrt_NonPositiveP_Throws()
    54	        {
    55	            var p = new BigInteger("0");
    56	            var x = new BigInteger("1");
    57	            Action act = () => Crypto.Math.ModSqrt(x, p);
    58	            act.Should().Throw<ArgumentException>()
    59	               .WithMessage("p must be positive");
    60	        }
    61	
    62	        [Fact]
    63	        public void ModSqrt_NegativeX_Throws()
    64	        {
    65	            // Upstream JS BigInt % keeps the sign of the dividend, so a
    66	            // negative x stays negative through "base = x % p" and then
    67	            // fails the squareRoot check. Mirror that.
    68	            var p = new BigInteger("7");
    69	            var x = new BigInteger("-4");
    70	            Action act = () => Crypto.Math.ModSqrt(x, p);
    71	            act.Should().Throw<InvalidOperationException>()
    72	               .WithMessage("could not find a modular square root");
    73	        }
    74	
    75	        [Fact]
    76	        public void ModSqrt_P256Prime_Works()
    77	        {
    78	            // P-256 prime. Pick any quadratic residue: 4 = 2^2.
    79	            var p = new BigInteger(CryptoConstants.P256_P);
    80	            var x = new BigInteger("4");
    81	            var root = Crypto.Math.ModSqrt(x, p);
    82	            root.Multiply(root).Mod(p).Equals(x).Should().BeTrue();
    83	        }
    84	
    85	        // ============================================================
    86	        // HKDF RFC 5869
    87	        // ============================================================
    88	
    89	        // RFC 5869 Test Case 1 (Basic test case with SHA-256)
    90	        [Fact]
    91	        public void Hkdf_Rfc5869_A1()
    92	        {
    93	            byte[] ikm = HexToBytes("0b0b0b0b0b0b0b0b0b0b0b0b0b0b0b0b0b0b0b0b0b0b");
    94	            byte[] salt = HexToBytes("000102030405060708090a0b0c");
    95	            byte[] info = HexToBytes("f0f1f2f3f4f5f6f7f8f9");
    96	            int L = 42;
    97	
    98	            byte[] prk = Crypto.Hkdf.Extract(salt, ikm);
    99	            byte[] okm = Crypto.Hkdf.Expand(prk, info, L);
   100	
   101	            BytesToHex(prk).Should().Be(
   102	                "077709362c2e32df0ddc3f0dc47bba6390b6c73bb50f9c3122ec844ad7c2b3e5");
   103	            BytesToHex(okm).Should().Be(
   104	                "3cb25f25faacd57a90434f64d0362f2a"
   105	                + "2d2d0a90cf1a5a4c5db02d56ecc4c5bf"
   106	                + "34007208d5b887185865");
   107	        }
   108	
   109	        // RFC 5869 Test Case 2 (Test with SHA-256 and longer inputs/outputs)
   110	        [Fact]
   111	        public void Hkdf_Rfc5869_A2()
   112	        {
   113	            byte[] ikm = HexToBytes(
   114	                "000102030405060708090a0b0c0d0e0f"
   115	                + "101112131415161718191a1b1c1d1e1f"
   116	                + "202122232425262728292a2b2c2d2e2f"
   117	                + "303132333435363738393a3b3c3d3e3f"
   118	                + "404142434445464748494a4b4c4d4e4f");
   119	            byte[] salt = HexToBytes(
   120	                "606162636465666768696a6b6c6d6e6f"
   121	                + "707172737475767778797a7b7c7d7e7f"
   122	                + "808182838485868788898a8b8c8d8e8f"
   123	                + "909192939495969798999a9b9c9d9e9f"
   124	                + "a0a1a2a3a4a5a6a7a8a9aaabacadaeaf");
   125	            byte[] info = HexToBytes(
   126	                "b0b1b2b3b4b5b6b7b8b9babbbcbdbebf"
   127	                + "c0c1c2c3c4c5c6c7c8c9cacbcccdcecf"
   128	                + "d0d1d2d3d4d5d6d7d8d9dadbdcdddedf"
   129	                + "e0e1e2e3e4e5e6e7e8e9eaebecedeeef"
   130	                + "f0f1f2f3f4f5f6f7f8f9fafbfcfdfeff");
   131	            int L = 82;
   132	
   133	            byte[] prk = Crypto.Hkdf.Extract(salt, ikm);
   134	            byte[] okm = Crypto.Hkdf.Expand(prk, info, L);
   135	
   136	            BytesToHex(prk).Should().Be(
   137	                "06a6b88c5853361a06104c9ceb35b45cef760014904671014a193f40c15fc244");
   138	            BytesToHex(okm).Should().Be(
   139	                "b11e398dc80327a1c8e7f78c596a4934"
   140	                + "4f012eda2d4efad8a050cc4c19afa97c"
   141	                + "59045a99cac7827271cb41c65e590e09"
   142	                + "da3275600c2f09b8367793a9aca3db71"
   143	                + "cc30c58179ec3e87c14c01d5c1f3434f"
   144	                + "1d87");
   145	        }
   146	
   147	        // RFC 5869 Test Case 3 (Test with SHA-256 and zero-length salt/info)
   148	        [Fact]
   149	        public void Hkdf_Rfc5869_A3()
   150	        {
   151	            byte[] ikm = HexToBytes("0b0b0b0b0b0b0b0b0b0b0b0b0b0b0b0b0b0b0b0b0b0b");
   152	            byte[] salt = Array.Empty<byte>();
   153	            byte[] info = Array.Empty<byte>();
   154	            int L = 42;
   155	
   156	            byte[] prk = Crypto.Hkdf.Extract(salt, ikm);
   157	            byte[] okm = Crypto.Hkdf.Expand(prk, info, L);
   158	
   159	            BytesToHex(prk).Should().Be(
   160	                "19ef24a32c717b167f33a91d6f648bdf96596776afdb6377ac434c1c293ccb04");
   161	            BytesToHex(okm).Should().Be(
   162	                "8da4e775a563c18f715f802a063c5a31"
   163	                + "b8a11f5c5ee1879ec3454e5f3c738d2d"
   164	                + "9d201395faa4b61a96c8");
   165	        }
   166	
   167	        [Fact]
   168	        public void Hkdf_Expand_Length0_ProducesEmpty()
   169	        {
   170	            byte[] prk = HexToBytes(
   171	                "077709362c2e32df0ddc3f0dc47bba6390b6c73bb50f9c3122ec844ad7c2b3e5");
   172	            byte[] okm = Crypto.Hkdf.Expand(prk, null!, 0);
   173	            okm.Should().BeEmpty();
   174	        }
   175	
   176	        [Fact]
   177	        public void Hkdf_Expand_OverMaxLength_Throws()
   178	        {
   179	            byte[] prk = new byte[32];
   180	            Action act = () => Crypto.Hkdf.Expand(prk, null!, 255 * 32 + 1);
   181	            act.Should().Throw<ArgumentException>();
   182	        }
   183	
   184	        [Fact]
   185	        public void Hkdf_Extract_NullSalt_UsesZeroSalt()
   186	        {
   187	            byte[] ikm = HexToBytes("0b0b0b0b0b0b0b0b0b0b0b0b0b0b0b0b0b0b0b0b0b0b");
   188	            byte[] prkA = Crypto.Hkdf.Extract(null!, ikm);
   189	            byte[] prkB = Crypto.Hkdf.Extract(new byte[32], ikm); // RFC default
   190	            prkA.Should().Equal(prkB);
   191	        }
   192	
   193	        // ============================================================
   194	        // CompressRawPublicKey / UncompressRawPublicKey
   195	        // ============================================================
   196	
   197	        [Fact]
   198	        public void CompressUncompress_Roundtrip_RandomKeys()
   199	        {
   200	            for (int i = 0; i < 8; i++)
   201	            {
   202	                var kp = Crypto.GenerateP256KeyPair();
   203	                byte[] uncompressed = Encoding.Uint8ArrayFromHexString(kp.PublicKeyUncompressed);
   204	                byte[] compressed = Crypto.CompressRawPublicKey(uncompressed);
   205	                compressed.Should().HaveCount(33);
   206	                compressed[0].Should().BeOneOf((byte)0x02, (byte)0x03);
   207	
   208	                byte[] recovered = Crypto.UncompressRawPublicKey(compressed);
   209	                recovered.Should().Equal(uncompressed);
   210	            }
   211	        }
   212	
   213	        [Fact]
   214	        public void CompressRawPublicKey_Permissive_OnAnyLength()
   215	        {
   216	            // Upstream behavior: slice + LSB-flip regardless of input shape.
   217	            // For a 65-byte uncompressed key we produce a 33-byte compressed key.
   218	            // For other lengths we produce a different-length output that
   219	            // upstream would also produce. Confirm we no longer throw on
   220	            // malformed input.
   221	            var raw = new byte[64];
   222	            raw[0] = 0x04;
   223	            raw[63] = 0x01;
   224	            Action act = () => Crypto.CompressRawPublicKey(raw);
   225	            act.Should().NotThrow();
   226	        }
   227	
   228	        [Fact]
   229	        public void UncompressRawPublicKey_BadPrefix_Throws()
   230	        {
   231	            var compressed = new byte[33];
   232	            compressed[0] = 0x05;
   233	            Action act = () => Crypto.UncompressRawPublicKey(compressed);
   234	            act.Should().Throw<ArgumentException>()
   235	               .WithMessage("failed to uncompress raw public key: invalid prefix");
   236	        }
   237	
   238	        [Fact]
   239	        public void UncompressRawPublicKey_WrongLength_Throws()
   240	        {
   241	            var compressed = new byte[32];
   242	            compressed[0] = 0x02;
   243	            Action act = () => Crypto.UncompressRawPublicKey(compressed);
   244	            act.Should().Throw<ArgumentException>()
   245	               .WithMessage("failed to uncompress raw public key: invalid length");
   246	        }
   247	
   248	        // ============================================================
   249	        // GetPublicKey / GenerateP256KeyPair
   250	        // ============================================================
   251	
   252	        [Fact]
   253	        public void GenerateP256KeyPair_LengthsAreCorrect()
   254	        {
   255	            var kp = Crypto.GenerateP256KeyPair();
   256	            kp.PrivateKey.Should().HaveLength(64);          // 32 bytes hex
   257	            kp.PublicKey.Should().HaveLength(66);            // 33 bytes hex
   258	            kp.PublicKeyUncompressed.Should().HaveLength(130); // 65 bytes hex
   259	            kp.PublicKey.Should().MatchRegex("^0[23][0-9a-f]{64}$");
   260	            kp.PublicKeyUncompressed.Should().StartWith("04");
     1	// ApiKeyStamperTests.cs — xunit tests for src/ApiKeyStamper.cs.
     2	//
     3	// Vector sources:
     4	//   codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/turnkey-api-key-stamper-0.5.0/ts-source/__fixtures__/api-key.{private,public}
     5	//   codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/turnkey-api-key-stamper-0.5.0/ts-source/__tests__/stamp-test.ts
     6	//
     7	// P-256 ECDSA signatures are not deterministic across libraries (noble uses
     8	// RFC 6979; BouncyCastle's HMacDsaKCalculator also uses RFC 6979 with the
     9	// same digest, so for identical input + key + nonce-source the signature
    10	// is deterministic). Tests therefore assert:
    11	//   - the header name == "X-Stamp"
    12	//   - the base64url-decoded JSON has keys ["publicKey", "scheme", "signature"]
    13	//   - publicKey matches the configured key
    14	//   - scheme == "SIGNATURE_SCHEME_TK_API_P256"
    15	//   - the signature is a valid DER-encoded ECDSA signature over SHA-256(content)
    16	//     under the configured public key
    17	
    18	using System;
    19	using System.IO;
    20	using System.Text;
    21	using System.Text.Json;
    22	using FluentAssertions;
    23	using Org.BouncyCastle.Asn1;
    24	using Org.BouncyCastle.Asn1.X9;
    25	using Org.BouncyCastle.Crypto.Parameters;
    26	using Org.BouncyCastle.Math;
    27	using Org.BouncyCastle.Security;
    28	using Xunit;
    29	
    30	namespace Turnkey.Tests
    31	{
    32	    public class ApiKeyStamperTests
    33	    {
    34	        // From upstream __fixtures__/api-key.{private,public}
    35	        private const string FixturePrivateKey =
    36	            "487f361ddfd73440e707f4daa6775b376859e8a3c9f29b3bb694a12927c0213c";
    37	        private const string FixturePublicKey =
    38	            "02f739f8c77b32f4d5f13265861febd76e7a9c61a1140d296b8c16302508870316";
    39	
    40	        [Fact]
    41	        public void Constructor_DefersValidation()
    42	        {
    43	            // Upstream `new ApiKeyStamper({apiPublicKey, apiPrivateKey})` only
    44	            // assigns fields; validation happens at sign time. C# mirrors
    45	            // that, including null tolerance.
    46	            Action act = () => new ApiKeyStamper(FixturePublicKey, "abcd");
    47	            act.Should().NotThrow();
    48	            Action act2 = () => new ApiKeyStamper(null!, null!);
    49	            act2.Should().NotThrow();
    50	        }
    51	
    52	        [Fact]
    53	        public void SignWithApiKey_RejectsBadKeyLength()
    54	        {
    55	            // Validation now occurs in SignWithApiKey -> Crypto.GetPublicKey.
    56	            var stamper = new ApiKeyStamper(FixturePublicKey, "abcd");
    57	            Action act = () => stamper.SignWithApiKey("payload");
    58	            act.Should().Throw<ArgumentException>()
    59	               .WithMessage("invalid P-256 private key: expected 32 bytes, got 2");
    60	        }
    61	
    62	        [Fact]
    63	        public void SignWithApiKey_RejectsZeroScalar()
    64	        {
    65	            string zero = new string('0', 64);
    66	            var stamper = new ApiKeyStamper(FixturePublicKey, zero);
    67	            Action act = () => stamper.SignWithApiKey("payload");
    68	            act.Should().Throw<ArgumentException>()
    69	               .WithMessage("invalid P-256 private key: scalar must be in [1, n - 1]");
    70	        }
    71	
    72	        [Fact]
    73	        public void Stamp_UpstreamFixture_ProducesValidWireBytes()
    74	        {
    75	            // Matches upstream __tests__/stamp-test.ts:6-37
    76	            var stamper = new ApiKeyStamper(FixturePublicKey, FixturePrivateKey);
    77	            const string messageToSign = "hello from TKHQ!";
    78	
    79	            var result = stamper.Stamp(messageToSign);
    80	
    81	            result.StampHeaderName.Should().Be("X-Stamp");
    82	
    83	            // Decode base64url -> JSON -> object.
    84	            string decodedJson = Encoding.DecodeBase64UrlToString(result.StampHeaderValue);
    85	
    86	            using var doc = JsonDocument.Parse(decodedJson);
    87	            var root = doc.RootElement;
    88	
    89	            // Keys must be exactly publicKey, scheme, signature in this order.
    90	            int i = 0;
    91	            string[] expectedKeys = { "publicKey", "scheme", "signature" };
    92	            foreach (var prop in root.EnumerateObject())
    93	            {
    94	                prop.Name.Should().Be(expectedKeys[i++]);
    95	            }
    96	            i.Should().Be(3);
    97	
    98	            root.GetProperty("publicKey").GetString().Should().Be(FixturePublicKey);
    99	            root.GetProperty("scheme").GetString().Should().Be("SIGNATURE_SCHEME_TK_API_P256");
   100	
   101	            // Verify the DER ECDSA signature against SHA-256(content) under the
   102	            // public key. This is the wire-format gate: if the signature isn't
   103	            // verifiable against the configured P-256 key, the stamp would be
   104	            // rejected by Turnkey's backend.
   105	            string signatureHex = root.GetProperty("signature").GetString()!;
   106	            byte[] signatureBytes = HexToBytes(signatureHex);
   107	            AssertSignatureVerifies(FixturePublicKey, messageToSign, signatureBytes);
   108	        }
   109	
   110	        [Fact]
   111	        public void SignWithApiKey_ProducesDerHexThatVerifies()
   112	        {
   113	            var stamper = new ApiKeyStamper(FixturePublicKey, FixturePrivateKey);
   114	            const string content = "any payload we care to sign";
   115	            string sigHex = stamper.SignWithApiKey(content);
   116	
   117	            // Output is DER hex; even length, starts with 30 (SEQUENCE tag).
   118	            (sigHex.Length % 2).Should().Be(0);
   119	            sigHex.Substring(0, 2).Should().Be("30");
   120	
   121	            AssertSignatureVerifies(FixturePublicKey, content, HexToBytes(sigHex));
   122	        }
   123	
   124	        [Fact]
   125	        public void SignWithApiKey_DeterministicWithRfc6979()
   126	        {
   127	            // RFC 6979 + same key + same content + same SHA-256(content) +
   128	            // same low-S normalization => signature is bit-identical across
   129	            // invocations (HMacDsaKCalculator is deterministic).
   130	            var stamper = new ApiKeyStamper(FixturePublicKey, FixturePrivateKey);
   131	            string a = stamper.SignWithApiKey("the same content");
   132	            string b = stamper.SignWithApiKey("the same content");
   133	            a.Should().Be(b);
   134	        }
   135	
   136	        [Fact]
   137	        public void SignWithApiKey_LowSEnforced()
   138	        {
   139	            // For RFC 6979 + low-S: the s value's high bit (top byte) must be
   140	            // strictly less than (n / 2 + 1). Verify by parsing the DER and
   141	            // checking the s integer is in the lower half of [1, n-1].
   142	            var stamper = new ApiKeyStamper(FixturePublicKey, FixturePrivateKey);
   143	            for (int i = 0; i < 8; i++)
   144	            {
   145	                string sigHex = stamper.SignWithApiKey("content_" + i);
   146	                var (r, s) = ParseDerEcdsa(HexToBytes(sigHex));
   147	
   148	                var curve = ECNamedCurveTable.GetByName(CryptoConstants.CURVE_NAME);
   149	                var halfN = curve.N.ShiftRight(1);
   150	                s.CompareTo(halfN).Should().BeLessOrEqualTo(0,
   151	                    "low-S requires s to be in (0, n/2]");
   152	                r.SignValue.Should().BeGreaterThan(0);
   153	                s.SignValue.Should().BeGreaterThan(0);
   154	            }
   155	        }
   156	
   157	        [Fact]
   158	        public void SignWithApiKey_WrongPublicKey_Throws()
   159	        {
   160	            // Mismatched public key (any other valid P-256 compressed key).
   161	            var other = Crypto.GenerateP256KeyPair();
   162	            Action act = () =>
   163	            {
   164	                var stamper = new ApiKeyStamper(other.PublicKey, FixturePrivateKey);
   165	                stamper.SignWithApiKey("payload");
   166	            };
   167	            act.Should().Throw<InvalidOperationException>()
   168	               .WithMessage("Bad API key. Expected to get public key*");
   169	        }
   170	
   171	        [Fact]
   172	        public void Stamp_HeaderConstantsAndScheme()
   173	        {
   174	            ApiKeyStamper.StampHeaderName.Should().Be("X-Stamp");
   175	            ApiKeyStamper.SignatureScheme.Should().Be("SIGNATURE_SCHEME_TK_API_P256");
   176	        }
   177	
   178	        [Fact]
   179	        public void StampHeaderValue_IsAsciiUrlSafe()
   180	        {
   181	            var stamper = new ApiKeyStamper(FixturePublicKey, FixturePrivateKey);
   182	            var result = stamper.Stamp("hello");
   183	            // base64url alphabet: A-Z a-z 0-9 - _
   184	            foreach (char c in result.StampHeaderValue)
   185	            {
   186	                bool ok = (c >= 'A' && c <= 'Z')
   187	                          || (c >= 'a' && c <= 'z')
   188	                          || (c >= '0' && c <= '9')
   189	                          || c == '-' || c == '_';
   190	                ok.Should().BeTrue($"char '{c}' is not in base64url alphabet");
   191	            }
   192	        }
   193	
   194	        // ============================================================
   195	        // Helpers
   196	        // ============================================================
   197	
   198	        private static byte[] HexToBytes(string hex) => Encoding.Uint8ArrayFromHexString(hex);
   199	
   200	        private static void AssertSignatureVerifies(string compressedPubHex, string content, byte[] derSignature)
   201	        {
   202	            byte[] payloadBytes = System.Text.Encoding.UTF8.GetBytes(content);
   203	
   204	            var digest = DigestUtilities.GetDigest("SHA-256");
   205	            digest.BlockUpdate(payloadBytes, 0, payloadBytes.Length);
   206	            var hash = new byte[digest.GetDigestSize()];
   207	            digest.DoFinal(hash, 0);
   208	
   209	            var curve = ECNamedCurveTable.GetByName(CryptoConstants.CURVE_NAME);
   210	            var domainParams = new ECDomainParameters(curve.Curve, curve.G, curve.N, curve.H, curve.GetSeed());
   211	
   212	            byte[] compressedPub = Encoding.Uint8ArrayFromHexString(compressedPubHex);
   213	            var point = curve.Curve.DecodePoint(compressedPub);
   214	            var publicKey = new ECPublicKeyParameters(point, domainParams);
   215	
   216	            var signer = SignerUtilities.GetSigner("NONEwithECDSA");
   217	            signer.Init(false, publicKey);
   218	            signer.BlockUpdate(hash, 0, hash.Length);
   219	            signer.VerifySignature(derSignature).Should().BeTrue();
   220	        }
   221	
   222	        private static (BigInteger R, BigInteger S) ParseDerEcdsa(byte[] der)
   223	        {
   224	            using var ms = new MemoryStream(der);
   225	            using var reader = new Asn1InputStream(ms);
   226	            var seq = (DerSequence)reader.ReadObject();
   227	            var r = ((DerInteger)seq[0]).Value;
   228	            var s = ((DerInteger)seq[1]).Value;
   229	            return (r, s);
   230	        }
   231	    }
   232	}
     1	// HttpTests.cs — xunit tests for src/Http.cs.
     2	
     3	using System;
     4	using System.Text.Json;
     5	using FluentAssertions;
     6	using Xunit;
     7	
     8	namespace Turnkey.Tests
     9	{
    10	    public class HttpTests
    11	    {
    12	        // Upstream api-key-stamper@0.5.0 fixture, reused to drive Http here so
    13	        // that the stamped bodies verify under a known public key.
    14	        private const string FixturePrivateKey =
    15	            "487f361ddfd73440e707f4daa6775b376859e8a3c9f29b3bb694a12927c0213c";
    16	        private const string FixturePublicKey =
    17	            "02f739f8c77b32f4d5f13265861febd76e7a9c61a1140d296b8c16302508870316";
    18	
    19	        private static Http MakeClient() => Http.FromTargetPrivateKey(FixturePrivateKey);
    20	
    21	        [Fact]
    22	        public void FromTargetPrivateKey_RejectsEmpty()
    23	        {
    24	            Action act = () => Http.FromTargetPrivateKey("");
    25	            act.Should().Throw<ArgumentException>();
    26	        }
    27	
    28	        [Fact]
    29	        public void FromTargetPrivateKey_RejectsInvalidHex()
    30	        {
    31	            Action act = () => Http.FromTargetPrivateKey("zz");
    32	            act.Should().Throw<ArgumentException>()
    33	               .WithMessage("cannot create uint8array from invalid hex string*");
    34	        }
    35	
    36	        [Fact]
    37	        public void GetHttpClient_RejectsEmptyArgs()
    38	        {
    39	            Action a1 = () => Http.GetHttpClient("", "abc");
    40	            a1.Should().Throw<ArgumentException>();
    41	            Action a2 = () => Http.GetHttpClient("abc", "");
    42	            a2.Should().Throw<ArgumentException>();
    43	        }
    44	
    45	        [Fact]
    46	        public void StampGetWhoami_ProducesCorrectUrlAndBody()
    47	        {
    48	            var http = MakeClient();
    49	            var req = http.StampGetWhoami("00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000");
    50	
    51	            req.Url.Should().Be("https://api.turnkey.com/public/v1/query/whoami");
    52	            req.Body.Should().Be("{\"organizationId\":\"00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000\"}");
    53	            req.Stamp.StampHeaderName.Should().Be("X-Stamp");
    54	            req.Stamp.StampHeaderValue.Should().NotBeNullOrEmpty();
    55	        }
    56	
    57	        [Fact]
    58	        public void StampGetWhoami_EmptyOrg_Throws()
    59	        {
    60	            var http = MakeClient();
    61	            Action act = () => http.StampGetWhoami("");
    62	            act.Should().Throw<ArgumentException>();
    63	        }
    64	
    65	        [Fact]
    66	        public void StampInitImportPrivateKey_ProducesCorrectUrlAndJson()
    67	        {
    68	            var http = MakeClient();
    69	            var body = new Http.InitImportPrivateKeyRequestBody
    70	            {
    71	                OrganizationId = "org-1",
    72	                Type = "ACTIVITY_TYPE_INIT_IMPORT_PRIVATE_KEY",
    73	                TimestampMs = "1729000000000",
    74	                Parameters = new Http.InitImportPrivateKeyParameters
    75	                {
    76	                    UserId = "user-1",
    77	                },
    78	            };
    79	
    80	            var req = http.StampInitImportPrivateKey(body);
    81	
    82	            req.Url.Should().Be("https://api.turnkey.com/public/v1/submit/init_import_private_key");
    83	
    84	            // Field order must be organizationId, type, timestampMs, parameters.
    85	            using var doc = JsonDocument.Parse(req.Body);
    86	            var keys = new System.Collections.Generic.List<string>();
    87	            foreach (var p in doc.RootElement.EnumerateObject())
    88	            {
    89	                keys.Add(p.Name);
    90	            }
    91	            keys.Should().ContainInOrder(new[] { "organizationId", "type", "timestampMs", "parameters" });
    92	
    93	            doc.RootElement.GetProperty("organizationId").GetString().Should().Be("org-1");
    94	            doc.RootElement.GetProperty("type").GetString().Should().Be("ACTIVITY_TYPE_INIT_IMPORT_PRIVATE_KEY");
    95	            doc.RootElement.GetProperty("timestampMs").GetString().Should().Be("1729000000000");
    96	            doc.RootElement.GetProperty("parameters").GetProperty("userId").GetString().Should().Be("user-1");
    97	        }
    98	
    99	        [Fact]
   100	        public void StampImportPrivateKey_ProducesArrayInBody()
   101	        {
   102	            var http = MakeClient();
   103	            var body = new Http.ImportPrivateKeyRequestBody
   104	            {
   105	                OrganizationId = "org-x",
   106	                Type = "ACTIVITY_TYPE_IMPORT_PRIVATE_KEY",
   107	                TimestampMs = "1729000000123",
   108	                Parameters = new Http.ImportPrivateKeyParameters
   109	                {
   110	                    UserId = "user-x",
   111	                    AddressFormats = new[] { "ADDRESS_FORMAT_ETHEREUM", "ADDRESS_FORMAT_SOLANA" },
   112	                    Curve = "CURVE_SECP256K1",
   113	                    EncryptedBundle = "{\"encappedPublic\":\"04...\",\"ciphertext\":\"ab\"}",
   114	                    PrivateKeyName = "my-key",
   115	                },
   116	            };
   117	
   118	            var req = http.StampImportPrivateKey(body);
   119	            req.Url.Should().Be("https://api.turnkey.com/public/v1/submit/import_private_key");
   120	
   121	            using var doc = JsonDocument.Parse(req.Body);
   122	            var parameters = doc.RootElement.GetProperty("parameters");
   123	            var formats = parameters.GetProperty("addressFormats");
   124	            formats.ValueKind.Should().Be(JsonValueKind.Array);
   125	            formats.GetArrayLength().Should().Be(2);
   126	            formats[0].GetString().Should().Be("ADDRESS_FORMAT_ETHEREUM");
   127	            formats[1].GetString().Should().Be("ADDRESS_FORMAT_SOLANA");
   128	            parameters.GetProperty("curve").GetString().Should().Be("CURVE_SECP256K1");
   129	        }
   130	
   131	        [Fact]
   132	        public void StampExportPrivateKey_ProducesCorrectShape()
   133	        {
   134	            var http = MakeClient();
   135	            var body = new Http.ExportPrivateKeyRequestBody
   136	            {
   137	                OrganizationId = "org-y",
   138	                Type = "ACTIVITY_TYPE_EXPORT_PRIVATE_KEY",
   139	                TimestampMs = "1",
   140	                Parameters = new Http.ExportPrivateKeyParameters
   141	                {
   142	                    PrivateKeyId = "pk-1",
   143	                    TargetPublicKey = FixturePublicKey,
   144	                },
   145	            };
   146	
   147	            var req = http.StampExportPrivateKey(body);
   148	            req.Url.Should().Be("https://api.turnkey.com/public/v1/submit/export_private_key");
   149	
   150	            using var doc = JsonDocument.Parse(req.Body);
   151	            var p = doc.RootElement.GetProperty("parameters");
   152	            p.GetProperty("privateKeyId").GetString().Should().Be("pk-1");
   153	            p.GetProperty("targetPublicKey").GetString().Should().Be(FixturePublicKey);
   154	        }
   155	
   156	        [Fact]
   157	        public void StampExportWalletAccount_ProducesCorrectShape()
   158	        {
   159	            var http = MakeClient();
   160	            var body = new Http.ExportWalletAccountRequestBody
   161	            {
   162	                OrganizationId = "org-z",
   163	                Type = "ACTIVITY_TYPE_EXPORT_WALLET_ACCOUNT",
   164	                TimestampMs = "2",
   165	                Parameters = new Http.ExportWalletAccountParameters
   166	                {
   167	                    Address = "0xabcdef",
   168	                    TargetPublicKey = FixturePublicKey,
   169	                },
   170	            };
   171	
   172	            var req = http.StampExportWalletAccount(body);
   173	            req.Url.Should().Be("https://api.turnkey.com/public/v1/submit/export_wallet_account");
   174	
   175	            using var doc = JsonDocument.Parse(req.Body);
   176	            var p = doc.RootElement.GetProperty("parameters");
   177	            p.GetProperty("address").GetString().Should().Be("0xabcdef");
   178	            p.GetProperty("targetPublicKey").GetString().Should().Be(FixturePublicKey);
   179	        }
   180	
   181	        [Fact]
   182	        public void Stamp_HeaderValueDecodesAndVerifies()
   183	        {
   184	            // End-to-end: stamp a whoami request and verify the X-Stamp header
   185	            // decodes to a stamp object that crypto-verifies over the body.
   186	            var http = MakeClient();
   187	            var req = http.StampGetWhoami("org-id");
   188	
   189	            string decodedJson = Encoding.DecodeBase64UrlToString(req.Stamp.StampHeaderValue);
   190	            using var doc = JsonDocument.Parse(decodedJson);
   191	            var root = doc.RootElement;
   192	            root.GetProperty("publicKey").GetString().Should().Be(FixturePublicKey);
   193	            root.GetProperty("scheme").GetString().Should().Be("SIGNATURE_SCHEME_TK_API_P256");
   194	            string signatureHex = root.GetProperty("signature").GetString()!;
   195	            signatureHex.Should().StartWith("30"); // DER SEQUENCE
   196	        }
   197	
   198	        [Fact]
   199	        public void StampNullBody_Throws()
   200	        {
   201	            var http = MakeClient();
   202	            Action a1 = () => http.StampInitImportPrivateKey(null!);
   203	            a1.Should().Throw<ArgumentNullException>();
   204	            Action a2 = () => http.StampImportPrivateKey(null!);
   205	            a2.Should().Throw<ArgumentNullException>();
   206	            Action a3 = () => http.StampExportPrivateKey(null!);
   207	            a3.Should().Throw<ArgumentNullException>();
   208	            Action a4 = () => http.StampExportWalletAccount(null!);
   209	            a4.Should().Throw<ArgumentNullException>();
   210	        }
   211	    }
   212	}
     1	# Test fixtures
     2	
     3	Each subdirectory groups fixtures for one production file, plus a
     4	provenance note explaining where the bytes came from.
     5	
     6	## Provenance levels
     7	
     8	- **upstream-test-vectors** — directly copied from the upstream
     9	  `__tests__/` directory inside `codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/`.
    10	- **rfc** — standardized test vectors (RFC 5869, RFC 9180, etc.).
    11	- **nist** — NIST CAVP P-256 vectors.
    12	- **turnkey-sample** — sample bundles published by Turnkey in their
    13	  developer docs (de-identified).
    14	- **node-generated** — bytes generated by running the pinned upstream Node
    15	  package inside `tests/Fixtures/Generators/`. The generator script,
    16	  Node version, and output hash are recorded so reviewers can re-run.
    17	
    18	## Layout
    19	
    20	```
    21	Fixtures/
    22	├── encoding/
    23	│   └── turnkey-encoding-vectors.json       — upstream-test-vectors
    24	├── crypto/                                  — (added with Crypto.cs port)
    25	├── api-key-stamper/                         — (added with ApiKeyStamper.cs port)
    26	└── http/                                    — (added with Http.cs port)
    27	```
    28	
    29	## Re-generating
    30	
    31	Fixtures sourced from upstream test files are stable as long as
    32	[`codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/`](../../codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/)
    33	is not bumped. If a pin is bumped, re-extract the upstream `__tests__/`
    34	into the matching fixture file and update its sha256 in
    35	`tarball-checksums.txt` next to the new tarball.

    52	// Envelope for serializing an encrypted message with its context.
    53	type Envelope = {
    54	  nonce: Uint8Array;
    55	  ephemeralSenderPublic: Uint8Array;
    56	  encryptedMessage: Uint8Array;
    57	};
    58	
    59	// schema for borsh serialization
    60	const EnvelopeSchema = {
    61	  struct: {

    63	    ephemeralSenderPublic: {
    64	      array: { type: "u8", len: UNCOMPRESSED_PUB_KEY_LENGTH_BYTES },

    66	    encryptedMessage: { array: { type: "u8" } },

    70	type Curve = "CURVE_SECP256K1" | "CURVE_P256";

    72	/**
    73	 * Get PublicKey function
    74	 * Derives public key from Uint8Array or hexstring private key
    75	 *
    76	 * @param {Uint8Array | string} privateKey - The Uint8Array or hexstring representation of a compressed private key.
    77	 * @param {boolean} isCompressed - Specifies whether to return a compressed or uncompressed public key. Defaults to true.
    78	 * @returns {Uint8Array} - The public key in Uin8Array representation.
    79	 */
    80	export const getPublicKey = (
    81	  privateKey: Uint8Array | string,
    82	  isCompressed: boolean = true,
    83	): Uint8Array => {
    84	  return p256.getPublicKey(privateKey, isCompressed);
    85	};
    86	
    87	/**
    88	 * HPKE Encrypt Function
    89	 * Encrypts data using Hybrid Public Key Encryption (HPKE) standard https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/rfc9180/.
    90	 *
    91	 * @param {HpkeEncryptParams} params - The encryption parameters including plain text, encapsulated key, and sender private key.
    92	 * @returns {Uint8Array} - The encrypted data.
    93	 */
    94	
    95	export const hpkeEncrypt = ({
    96	  plainTextBuf,
    97	  targetKeyBuf,
    98	}: HpkeEncryptParams): Uint8Array => {
    99	  try {
   100	    // Standard HPKE Mode (Ephemeral Key Pair)
   101	    const ephemeralKeyPair = generateP256KeyPair();
   102	    const senderPrivBuf = uint8ArrayFromHexString(ephemeralKeyPair.privateKey);
   103	    const senderPubBuf = uint8ArrayFromHexString(
   104	      ephemeralKeyPair.publicKeyUncompressed,
   105	    );
   106	
   107	    const aad = buildAdditionalAssociatedData(senderPubBuf, targetKeyBuf);
   108	
   109	    // Step 1: Generate Shared Secret
   110	    const ss = deriveSS(targetKeyBuf, uint8ArrayToHexString(senderPrivBuf!));
   111	
   112	    // Step 2: Generate the KEM context
   113	    const kemContext = getKemContext(
   114	      senderPubBuf,
   115	      uint8ArrayToHexString(targetKeyBuf),
   116	    );
   117	
   118	    // Step 3: Build the HKDF inputs for key derivation
   119	    let ikm = buildLabeledIkm(LABEL_EAE_PRK, ss, SUITE_ID_1);
   120	    let info = buildLabeledInfo(
   121	      LABEL_SHARED_SECRET,
   122	      kemContext,
   123	      SUITE_ID_1,
   124	      32,
   125	    );
   126	    const sharedSecret = extractAndExpand(new Uint8Array([]), ikm, info, 32);
   127	
   128	    // Step 4: Derive the AES key
   129	    ikm = buildLabeledIkm(LABEL_SECRET, new Uint8Array([]), SUITE_ID_2);
   130	    info = AES_KEY_INFO;
   131	    const key = extractAndExpand(sharedSecret, ikm, info, 32);
   132	
   133	    // Step 5: Derive the initialization vector
   134	    info = IV_INFO;
   135	    const iv = extractAndExpand(sharedSecret, ikm, info, 12);
   136	
   137	    // Step 6: Encrypt the data using AES-GCM
   138	    const encryptedData = aesGcmEncrypt(plainTextBuf, key, iv, aad);
   139	
   140	    // Step 7: Concatenate the encapsulated key and the encrypted data for output
   141	    const compressedSenderBuf = compressRawPublicKey(senderPubBuf);
   142	    const result = new Uint8Array(
   143	      compressedSenderBuf.length + encryptedData.length,
   144	    );
   145	    result.set(compressedSenderBuf, 0);
   146	    result.set(encryptedData, compressedSenderBuf.length);
   147	
   148	    return result;
   149	  } catch (error) {
   150	    throw new Error(`Unable to perform hpkeEncrypt: ${error}`);
   151	  }
   152	};
   153	
   154	/**
   155	 * HPKE Encrypt Function
   156	 * Encrypts data using Authenticated ,Hybrid Public Key Encryption (HPKE) standard https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/rfc9180/.
   157	 *
   158	 * @param {HpkeAuthEncryptParams} params - The encryption parameters including plain text, encapsulated key, and sender private key.
   159	 * @returns {Uint8Array} - The encrypted data.
   160	 */
   161	
   162	export const hpkeAuthEncrypt = ({
   163	  plainTextBuf,
   164	  targetKeyBuf,
   165	  senderPriv,
   166	}: HpkeAuthEncryptParams): Uint8Array => {
   167	  try {
   168	    // Authenticated HPKE Mode
   169	    const senderPrivBuf = uint8ArrayFromHexString(senderPriv);
   170	    const senderPubBuf = getPublicKey(senderPriv, false);
   171	
   172	    const aad = buildAdditionalAssociatedData(senderPubBuf, targetKeyBuf);
   173	
   174	    // Step 1: Generate Shared Secret
   175	    const ss = deriveSS(targetKeyBuf, uint8ArrayToHexString(senderPrivBuf!));
   176	
   177	    // Step 2: Generate the KEM context
   178	    const kemContext = getKemContext(
   179	      senderPubBuf,
   180	      uint8ArrayToHexString(targetKeyBuf),
   181	    );
   182	
   183	    // Step 3: Build the HKDF inputs for key derivation
   184	    let ikm = buildLabeledIkm(LABEL_EAE_PRK, ss, SUITE_ID_1);

   186	      LABEL_SHARED_SECRET,

   189	      32,
   190	    );

   192	
   193	    // Step 4: Derive the AES key
   194	    ikm = buildLabeledIkm(LABEL_SECRET, new Uint8Array([]), SUITE_ID_2);
   195	    info = AES_KEY_INFO;
   196	    const key = extractAndExpand(sharedSecret, ikm, info, 32);
   197	
   198	    // Step 5: Derive the initialization vector
   199	    info = IV_INFO;
   200	    const iv = extractAndExpand(sharedSecret, ikm, info, 12);
   201	
   202	    // Step 6: Encrypt the data using AES-GCM
   203	    const encryptedData = aesGcmEncrypt(plainTextBuf, key, iv, aad);
   204	
   205	    // Step 7: Concatenate the encapsulated key and the encrypted data for output
   206	    const compressedSenderBuf = compressRawPublicKey(senderPubBuf);

   208	      compressedSenderBuf.length + encryptedData.length,
   209	    );
   210	    result.set(compressedSenderBuf, 0);
   211	    result.set(encryptedData, compressedSenderBuf.length);
   212	    return result;
   213	  } catch (error) {
   214	    throw new Error(`Unable to perform hpkeEncrypt: ${error}`);
   215	  }
   216	};

   218	/**
   219	 * Encrypt a message to a quorum key. Algorithm originally implemented in qos here: https://github.com/tkhq/qos/blob/ae01904c756107f850aea42000137ef124df3fe4/src/qos_p256/src/encrypt.rs#L123
   220	 * Returns a borsh serialized encrypted Envelope which is the nonce + ephemeralSenderPublicKey + encryptedMessage
   221	 * This function creates an ephemeral key, creates a shared secret with the recipient targetPublicKeyUncompressed
   222	 * creates additional associated data which follows the form: sender_public||sender_public_len||receiver_public||receiver_public_len
   223	 * encrypts using aes-gcm-256 with a SHA-512 HMAC over the QOS_ENCRYPTION_HMAC_MESSAGE literally: "qos_encryption_hmac_message"
   224	 * inserts and returns the necessary information in a borsh serialized envelope as described above
   225	 * This encryption function is meant to be used with this decryption function in QOS: https://github.com/tkhq/qos/blob/ae01904c756107f850aea42000137ef124df3fe4/src/qos_p256/src/encrypt.rs#L52
   226	 *
   227	 * @param {Uint8Array} targetPublicKeyUncompressed - The P256 uncompressed public key to encrypt the message to
   228	 * @param {Uint8Array} message - The message to encrypt to targetPublicKeyUncompressed
   229	 * @returns {Uint8Array} - A borsh serialized envelope containing the nonce + ephemeralSenderPublicKey + encrypted message
   230	 */

   232	  targetPublicKeyUncompressed: Uint8Array,

   251	  const aad = createAdditionalAssociatedData(
   252	    uint8ArrayFromHexString(ephemeralSenderPublic),
   253	    targetPublicKeyUncompressed,
   254	  );

   257	  const alg: AesGcmParams = {
   258	    name: "AES-GCM",
   259	    iv: nonce,

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/turnkey-api-key-stamper-0.5.0/ts-source/index.ts | sed -n '1,120p' && nl -ba codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/turnkey-api-key-stamper-0.5.0/ts-source/purejs.ts | sed -n '1,120p'" in /Users/takeshi/Kyuzan/src/turnkey-sdk-csharp
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	/// <reference lib="dom" />
     2	import { stringToBase64urlString } from "@turnkey/encoding";
     3	
     4	// Header name for an API key stamp
     5	const stampHeaderName = "X-Stamp";
     6	
     7	export type Runtime = "browser" | "node" | "purejs";
     8	
     9	export type TApiKeyStamperConfig = {
    10	  apiPublicKey: string;
    11	  apiPrivateKey: string;
    12	  runtimeOverride?: Runtime | undefined;
    13	};
    14	
    15	// `window.document` ensures that we're in a browser context
    16	// and `crypto.subtle` ensures that it supports the web crypto APIs
    17	// Inspired by https://github.com/flexdinesh/browser-or-node/blob/master/src/index.ts
    18	const isCryptoEnabledBrowser: boolean =
    19	  typeof window !== "undefined" &&
    20	  typeof window.document !== "undefined" &&
    21	  typeof crypto !== "undefined" &&
    22	  typeof crypto.subtle !== "undefined";
    23	
    24	// We check `process.versions.node`
    25	// Taken from https://github.com/flexdinesh/browser-or-node/blob/master/src/index.ts
    26	const isNode: boolean =
    27	  typeof process !== "undefined" &&
    28	  process.versions != null &&
    29	  process.versions.node != null;
    30	
    31	const detectRuntime = (): Runtime => {
    32	  if (isCryptoEnabledBrowser) {
    33	    return "browser";
    34	  }
    35	
    36	  if (isNode) {
    37	    return "node";
    38	  }
    39	
    40	  // If we don't have NodeJS or web crypto at our disposal, default to pure JS implementation
    41	  // This is the case for old browsers and react native environments
    42	  return "purejs";
    43	};
    44	
    45	/**
    46	 * Signature function abstracting the differences between NodeJS and web environments for signing with API keys.
    47	 */
    48	export const signWithApiKey = async (
    49	  input: {
    50	    content: string;
    51	    publicKey: string;
    52	    privateKey: string;
    53	  },
    54	  runtimeOverride?: Runtime | undefined,
    55	): Promise<string> => {
    56	  const runtime = runtimeOverride ?? detectRuntime();
    57	
    58	  switch (runtime) {
    59	    case "browser":
    60	      return (await import("./webcrypto")).signWithApiKey(input);
    61	    case "node":
    62	      return (await import("./nodecrypto")).signWithApiKey(input);
    63	    case "purejs":
    64	      return (await import("./purejs")).signWithApiKey(input);
    65	    default:
    66	      throw new Error(`Unsupported runtime: ${runtime}`);
    67	  }
    68	};
    69	
    70	/**
    71	 * Stamper to use with `@turnkey/http`'s `TurnkeyClient`
    72	 */
    73	export class ApiKeyStamper {
    74	  apiPublicKey: string;
    75	  apiPrivateKey: string;
    76	  runtimeOverride?: Runtime | undefined;
    77	
    78	  constructor(config: TApiKeyStamperConfig) {
    79	    this.apiPublicKey = config.apiPublicKey;
    80	    this.apiPrivateKey = config.apiPrivateKey;
    81	    this.runtimeOverride = config.runtimeOverride;
    82	  }
    83	
    84	  async stamp(payload: string) {
    85	    const signature = await signWithApiKey(
    86	      {
    87	        publicKey: this.apiPublicKey,
    88	        privateKey: this.apiPrivateKey,
    89	        content: payload,
    90	      },
    91	      this.runtimeOverride,
    92	    );
    93	
    94	    const stamp = {
    95	      publicKey: this.apiPublicKey,
    96	      scheme: "SIGNATURE_SCHEME_TK_API_P256",
    97	      signature,
    98	    };
    99	
   100	    return {
   101	      stampHeaderName,
   102	      stampHeaderValue: stringToBase64urlString(JSON.stringify(stamp)),
   103	    };
   104	  }
   105	}
   106	
   107	export { pointDecode } from "./tink/elliptic_curves";
     1	import { p256 } from "@noble/curves/p256";
     2	import { createHash } from "sha256-uint8array";
     3	import { uint8ArrayToHexString } from "@turnkey/encoding";
     4	
     5	export const signWithApiKey = async (input: {
     6	  content: string;
     7	  publicKey: string;
     8	  privateKey: string;
     9	}): Promise<string> => {
    10	  const publicKey = p256.getPublicKey(input.privateKey, true);
    11	
    12	  // Public key in the usual 02 or 03 + 64 hex digits
    13	  const publicKeyString = uint8ArrayToHexString(publicKey);
    14	
    15	  if (publicKeyString != input.publicKey) {
    16	    throw new Error(
    17	      `Bad API key. Expected to get public key ${input.publicKey}, got ${publicKeyString}`,
    18	    );
    19	  }
    20	
    21	  const hash = createHash().update(input.content).digest();
    22	  const signature = p256.sign(hash, input.privateKey);
    23	  return signature.toDERHex();
    24	};

    73	export async function request<

   156	    }
   157	  }
   158	
   159	  return url;
   160	}

   206	function stableStringify(input: Record<string, any>): string {
   207	  return JSON.stringify(input);
   208	}
   209	
   210	/**
   211	 * Seals and stamps the request body with your Turnkey API credentials.
   212	 *
   213	 * You can either:
   214	 * - Before calling `sealAndStampRequestBody(...)`, initialize with your Turnkey API credentials via `init(...)`
   215	 * - Or, provide `apiPublicKey` and `apiPrivateKey` here as arguments
   216	 */
   217	export async function sealAndStampRequestBody(input: {
   218	  body: Record<string, any>;
   219	  apiPublicKey?: string;
   220	  apiPrivateKey?: string;
   221	}): Promise<{
   222	  sealedBody: string;
   223	  xStamp: string;
   224	}> {
   225	  const { body } = input;
   226	  let { apiPublicKey, apiPrivateKey } = input;
   227	
   228	  if (!apiPublicKey) {
   229	    const config = getConfig();
   230	    apiPublicKey = config.apiPublicKey;
   231	  }
   232	
   233	  if (!apiPrivateKey) {
   234	    const config = getConfig();
   235	    apiPrivateKey = config.apiPrivateKey;
   236	  }
   237	
   238	  const sealedBody = stableStringify(body);
   239	  const signature = await signWithApiKey({
   240	    content: sealedBody,
   241	    privateKey: apiPrivateKey,
   242	    publicKey: apiPublicKey,
   243	  });
   244	  const sealedStamp = stableStringify({
   245	    publicKey: apiPublicKey,
   246	    scheme: "SIGNATURE_SCHEME_TK_API_P256",
   247	    signature: signature,
   248	  });
   249	
   250	  const xStamp = stringToBase64urlString(sealedStamp);
   251	
   252	  return {
   253	    sealedBody,
   254	    xStamp,
   255	  };
   256	}

   258	// Check if the client is an instance of TurnkeyClient. We check the name field here since the 'instanceof' operator does not work across if the http client isn't EXACTLY the same (mismatching versions).
   259	export function isHttpClient(client: any): client is TurnkeyClient {
   260	  return client?.name === "TurnkeyClient";
   261	}

   274	};

   276	/**

   280	 * Returns a JSON string of an encrypted bundle, separating out the cipher text and the sender public key
   281	 *
   282	 * @param {Uint8Array} encryptedBuf - The result of hpkeAuthEncrypt or hpkeEncrypt
   283	 * @returns {string} - A JSON string with "encappedPublic" and "ciphertext"
   284	 */
   285	
   286	export const formatHpkeBuf = (encryptedBuf: Uint8Array): string => {
   287	  const compressedSenderBuf = encryptedBuf.slice(0, 33);
   288	  const encryptedData = encryptedBuf.slice(33);
   289	
   290	  const encappedKeyBufHex = uint8ArrayToHexString(
   291	    uncompressRawPublicKey(compressedSenderBuf),
   292	  );
   293	  const ciphertextHex = uint8ArrayToHexString(encryptedData);
   294	
   295	  return JSON.stringify({
   296	    encappedPublic: encappedKeyBufHex,
   297	    ciphertext: ciphertextHex,
   298	  });
   299	};
   300

codex-crypto-reviews/Encoding.cs-r1-20260523.md:308:src/turnkey-sdk-csharp.csproj:12:    <Description>Unofficial / community-maintained Turnkey SDK for .NET by Kyuzan Inc. Not affiliated with Turnkey, Inc. 1:1 logical port of @turnkey/crypto v2.8.8, @turnkey/http v3.16.0, @turnkey/api-key-stamper v0.5.0, and @turnkey/encoding v0.6.0 (peak-pinned versions). Provides P-256 ECDSA / HPKE / HKDF / API key stamping primitives for .NET. BouncyCastle 2.5.0 is used only for primitives; HPKE / HKDF / bundle parsing are direct ports of the upstream Turnkey TypeScript logic.</Description>

codex-crypto-reviews/Encoding.cs-r1-20260523.md:3778:- Tarball sha256: `2cf9e6ee1f47ac7e3cc3e644cdb0e3e112c906a6ea1af737777f4658b73fb7bc`
codex-crypto-reviews/Encoding.cs-r1-20260523.md:3807:- TS `reduce` string building -> `StringBuilder`: same emitted characters/bytes.
codex-crypto-reviews/Encoding.cs-r1-20260523.md:3809:- Pure JS `btoa` over 0..255 code units -> code-point guard plus `Convert.ToBase64String`: same base64 bytes for valid upstream inputs.
codex-crypto-reviews/Encoding.cs-r1-20260523.md:3810:- Regex and `parseInt` for validated hex -> `Regex` plus `Convert.ToByte`: same bytes for `uint8ArrayFromHexString` valid inputs.
codex-crypto-reviews/Encoding.cs-r1-20260523.md:3828:| `src/Encoding.cs:431-435` | `ts-source/bs58.ts:36` | Invalid base58 inputs throw C# `ArgumentException` with custom text; upstream delegates to `bs58@6.0.0` and likely exposes dependency-specific error text/type. Valid bytes match. | Either match upstream dependency error messages or explicitly document that invalid-input exception text is not a parity surface. |
codex-crypto-reviews/Encoding.cs-r1-20260523.md:3829:| `src/Encoding.cs:490-507` | `ts-source/bs58check.ts:36` | Invalid/too-short/checksum-failing Base58Check inputs throw C# custom messages; upstream delegates to `bs58check@4.0.0` dependency behavior. Valid payload/checksum bytes match. | Either match upstream dependency error behavior or document exception text as intentionally .NET-native. |
codex-crypto-reviews/Encoding.cs-r1-20260523.md:3835:I did verify that `tests/EncodingTests.cs` uses inline vectors copied from `ts-source/__tests__/index-test.ts` for the main hex/base64 cases, and I ran Node `Buffer` checks for the base64url expected values. The Base58/Base58Check tests are local known-vector/roundtrip tests, not generated fixture files from the pinned upstream package.
codex-crypto-reviews/Encoding.cs-r1-20260523.md:3851:- Tarball sha256: `2cf9e6ee1f47ac7e3cc3e644cdb0e3e112c906a6ea1af737777f4658b73fb7bc`
codex-crypto-reviews/Encoding.cs-r1-20260523.md:3880:- TS `reduce` string building -> `StringBuilder`: same emitted characters/bytes.
codex-crypto-reviews/Encoding.cs-r1-20260523.md:3882:- Pure JS `btoa` over 0..255 code units -> code-point guard plus `Convert.ToBase64String`: same base64 bytes for valid upstream inputs.
codex-crypto-reviews/Encoding.cs-r1-20260523.md:3883:- Regex and `parseInt` for validated hex -> `Regex` plus `Convert.ToByte`: same bytes for `uint8ArrayFromHexString` valid inputs.
codex-crypto-reviews/Encoding.cs-r1-20260523.md:3901:| `src/Encoding.cs:431-435` | `ts-source/bs58.ts:36` | Invalid base58 inputs throw C# `ArgumentException` with custom text; upstream delegates to `bs58@6.0.0` and likely exposes dependency-specific error text/type. Valid bytes match. | Either match upstream dependency error messages or explicitly document that invalid-input exception text is not a parity surface. |
codex-crypto-reviews/Encoding.cs-r1-20260523.md:3902:| `src/Encoding.cs:490-507` | `ts-source/bs58check.ts:36` | Invalid/too-short/checksum-failing Base58Check inputs throw C# custom messages; upstream delegates to `bs58check@4.0.0` dependency behavior. Valid payload/checksum bytes match. | Either match upstream dependency error behavior or document exception text as intentionally .NET-native. |
codex-crypto-reviews/Encoding.cs-r1-20260523.md:3908:I did verify that `tests/EncodingTests.cs` uses inline vectors copied from `ts-source/__tests__/index-test.ts` for the main hex/base64 cases, and I ran Node `Buffer` checks for the base64url expected values. The Base58/Base58Check tests are local known-vector/roundtrip tests, not generated fixture files from the pinned upstream package.

codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r1-20260523.md:651:src/turnkey-sdk-csharp.csproj:12:    <Description>Unofficial / community-maintained Turnkey SDK for .NET by Kyuzan Inc. Not affiliated with Turnkey, Inc. 1:1 logical port of @turnkey/crypto v2.8.8, @turnkey/http v3.16.0, @turnkey/api-key-stamper v0.5.0, and @turnkey/encoding v0.6.0 (peak-pinned versions). Provides P-256 ECDSA / HPKE / HKDF / API key stamping primitives for .NET. BouncyCastle 2.5.0 is used only for primitives; HPKE / HKDF / bundle parsing are direct ports of the upstream Turnkey TypeScript logic.</Description>

codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r1-20260523.md:1400:src/turnkey-sdk-csharp.csproj:12:    <Description>Unofficial / community-maintained Turnkey SDK for .NET by Kyuzan Inc. Not affiliated with Turnkey, Inc. 1:1 logical port of @turnkey/crypto v2.8.8, @turnkey/http v3.16.0, @turnkey/api-key-stamper v0.5.0, and @turnkey/encoding v0.6.0 (peak-pinned versions). Provides P-256 ECDSA / HPKE / HKDF / API key stamping primitives for .NET. BouncyCastle 2.5.0 is used only for primitives; HPKE / HKDF / bundle parsing are direct ports of the upstream Turnkey TypeScript logic.</Description>

codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r1-20260523.md:2469:codex-crypto-reviews/Encoding.cs-r1-20260523.md:3828:| `src/Encoding.cs:431-435` | `ts-source/bs58.ts:36` | Invalid base58 inputs throw C# `ArgumentException` with custom text; upstream delegates to `bs58@6.0.0` and likely exposes dependency-specific error text/type. Valid bytes match. | Either match upstream dependency error messages or explicitly document that invalid-input exception text is not a parity surface. |
codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r1-20260523.md:2470:codex-crypto-reviews/Encoding.cs-r1-20260523.md:3829:| `src/Encoding.cs:490-507` | `ts-source/bs58check.ts:36` | Invalid/too-short/checksum-failing Base58Check inputs throw C# custom messages; upstream delegates to `bs58check@4.0.0` dependency behavior. Valid payload/checksum bytes match. | Either match upstream dependency error behavior or document exception text as intentionally .NET-native. |
codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r1-20260523.md:2472:codex-crypto-reviews/Encoding.cs-r1-20260523.md:3835:I did verify that `tests/EncodingTests.cs` uses inline vectors copied from `ts-source/__tests__/index-test.ts` for the main hex/base64 cases, and I ran Node `Buffer` checks for the base64url expected values. The Base58/Base58Check tests are local known-vector/roundtrip tests, not generated fixture files from the pinned upstream package.
codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r1-20260523.md:2475:codex-crypto-reviews/Encoding.cs-r1-20260523.md:3882:- Pure JS `btoa` over 0..255 code units -> code-point guard plus `Convert.ToBase64String`: same base64 bytes for valid upstream inputs.

Valid bytes match. | Either match upstream dependency error messages or explicitly document that invalid-input exception text is not a parity surface. |
codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r1-20260523.md:2480:codex-crypto-reviews/Encoding.cs-r1-20260523.md:3902:| `src/Encoding.cs:490-507` | `ts-source/bs58check.ts:36` | Invalid/too-short/checksum-failing Base58Check inputs throw C# custom messages; upstream delegates to `bs58check@4.0.0` dependency behavior. codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r1-20260523.md:2694:codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r1-20260523.md:651:src/turnkey-sdk-csharp.csproj:12:    <Description>Unofficial / community-maintained Turnkey SDK for .NET by Kyuzan Inc. Not affiliated with Turnkey, Inc. 1:1 logical port of @turnkey/crypto v2.8.8, @turnkey/http v3.16.0, @turnkey/api-key-stamper v0.5.0, and @turnkey/encoding v0.6.0 (peak-pinned versions). Provides P-256 ECDSA / HPKE / HKDF / API key stamping primitives for .NET. BouncyCastle 2.5.0 is used only for primitives; HPKE / HKDF / bundle parsing are direct ports of the upstream Turnkey TypeScript logic.</Description>

codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r1-20260523.md:3676:codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5482:5190:| `src/Crypto.cs:579-582` | `ts-source/crypto.ts:286-299` | C# rejects buffers `<= 33`; TS slices first 33 bytes and allows downstream behavior. A valid compressed key plus empty ciphertext would stringify upstream but throw in C#. | Match TS slicing behavior, or only reject `< 33` if defensive validation is desired. |
codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r1-20260523.md:3681:codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5488:5196:| `src/Crypto.cs:827-830` | `ts-source/turnkey.ts:164-180` | SOLANA export output is wrong: C# Base58-encodes only 32 decrypted private-key bytes; upstream derives Ed25519 public key, concatenates 32 private + 32 public bytes, then Base58-encodes 64 bytes. | Derive Ed25519 public key and encode the 64-byte concatenation. |
codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r1-20260523.md:3686:codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5541:- `DecodeKey` omits TS `console.warn` for unknown key format: return bytes still follow upstream hex fallback.
codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r1-20260523.md:3692:codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5567:Result: fixture gate is not satisfiable as “all fixtures match” for `Crypto.cs`; there are no pinned Node-generated Crypto fixtures to compare. codex-crypto-reviews/Http.cs-r1-20260523.md:10209:./codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r3-20260523.md:2053:codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r3-20260523.md:1393:    12	    "byte_level_assertion_strategy": "Exact Node-generated byte snapshots would require running the upstream npm package with its full node_modules; the tarball ships only built JS + declarations. Instead, ApiKeyStamperTests asserts wire-format equivalence by: (1) reproducing the upstream stamp-test.ts shape (decoded JSON key order = ['publicKey', 'scheme', 'signature']; scheme constant; publicKey equality); (2) crypto-verifying the DER ECDSA signature against SHA-256(content) under the upstream fixture public key; (3) asserting RFC 6979 determinism (same content -> same signature); (4) asserting low-S (s in (0, n/2]). This combination is sufficient to guarantee the stamp is accepted by Turnkey's backend, which is the wire-format goal."

codex-crypto-reviews/Http.cs-r1-20260523.md:10284:./codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r3-20260523.md:2162:codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r1-20260523.md:651:src/turnkey-sdk-csharp.csproj:12:    <Description>Unofficial / community-maintained Turnkey SDK for .NET by Kyuzan Inc. Not affiliated with Turnkey, Inc. 1:1 logical port of @turnkey/crypto v2.8.8, @turnkey/http v3.16.0, @turnkey/api-key-stamper v0.5.0, and @turnkey/encoding v0.6.0 (peak-pinned versions). Provides P-256 ECDSA / HPKE / HKDF / API key stamping primitives for .NET. BouncyCastle 2.5.0 is used only for primitives; HPKE / HKDF / bundle parsing are direct ports of the upstream Turnkey TypeScript logic.</Description>

codex-crypto-reviews/Http.cs-r1-20260523.md:10513:./codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r3-20260523.md:2571:codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r1-20260523.md:2694:codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r1-20260523.md:651:src/turnkey-sdk-csharp.csproj:12:    <Description>Unofficial / community-maintained Turnkey SDK for .NET by Kyuzan Inc. Not affiliated with Turnkey, Inc. 1:1 logical port of @turnkey/crypto v2.8.8, @turnkey/http v3.16.0, @turnkey/api-key-stamper v0.5.0, and @turnkey/encoding v0.6.0 (peak-pinned versions). Provides P-256 ECDSA / HPKE / HKDF / API key stamping primitives for .NET. BouncyCastle 2.5.0 is used only for primitives; HPKE / HKDF / bundle parsing are direct ports of the upstream Turnkey TypeScript logic.</Description>

codex-crypto-reviews/Http.cs-r1-20260523.md:10604:./codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r3-20260523.md:2824:codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r1-20260523.md:5535:./src/turnkey-sdk-csharp.csproj:12:    <Description>Unofficial / community-maintained Turnkey SDK for .NET by Kyuzan Inc. Not affiliated with Turnkey, Inc. 1:1 logical port of @turnkey/crypto v2.8.8, @turnkey/http v3.16.0, @turnkey/api-key-stamper v0.5.0, and @turnkey/encoding v0.6.0 (peak-pinned versions). Provides P-256 ECDSA / HPKE / HKDF / API key stamping primitives for .NET. BouncyCastle 2.5.0 is used only for primitives; HPKE / HKDF / bundle parsing are direct ports of the upstream Turnkey TypeScript logic.</Description>

codex-crypto-reviews/Http.cs-r1-20260523.md:10619:./codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r3-20260523.md:2855:codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r1-20260523.md:6084:- `tests/ApiKeyStamperTests.cs` uses upstream fixture constants inline, but it does not assert exact Node-generated stamp bytes. It verifies JSON key order, constants, DER shape, signature validity, determinism, and low-S behavior.

codex-crypto-reviews/Http.cs-r1-20260523.md:10624:./codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r3-20260523.md:2862:codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r1-20260523.md:6135:- No fixture under `tests/Fixtures/` currently exercises `src/ApiKeyStamper.cs`. `tests/Fixtures/README.md` lists `api-key-stamper/`, but that directory is absent.
codex-crypto-reviews/Http.cs-r1-20260523.md:10625:./codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r3-20260523.md:2863:codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r1-20260523.md:6136:- `tests/ApiKeyStamperTests.cs` uses upstream fixture constants inline, but it does not assert exact Node-generated stamp bytes. It verifies JSON key order, constants, DER shape, signature validity, determinism, and low-S behavior.

codex-crypto-reviews/Http.cs-r1-20260523.md:10633:./codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r3-20260523.md:3064:src/turnkey-sdk-csharp.csproj:12:    <Description>Unofficial / community-maintained Turnkey SDK for .NET by Kyuzan Inc. Not affiliated with Turnkey, Inc. 1:1 logical port of @turnkey/crypto v2.8.8, @turnkey/http v3.16.0, @turnkey/api-key-stamper v0.5.0, and @turnkey/encoding v0.6.0 (peak-pinned versions). Provides P-256 ECDSA / HPKE / HKDF / API key stamping primitives for .NET. BouncyCastle 2.5.0 is used only for primitives; HPKE / HKDF / bundle parsing are direct ports of the upstream Turnkey TypeScript logic.</Description>

codex-crypto-reviews/Http.cs-r1-20260523.md:10646:./codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r3-20260523.md:3179:tests/Fixtures/api-key-stamper/turnkey-api-key-stamper-vectors.json:5:    "source_test": "codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/turnkey-api-key-stamper-0.5.0/ts-source/__tests__/stamp-test.ts",
codex-crypto-reviews/Http.cs-r1-20260523.md:10647:./codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r3-20260523.md:3180:tests/Fixtures/api-key-stamper/turnkey-api-key-stamper-vectors.json:6:    "package": "@turnkey/api-key-stamper@0.5.0",
codex-crypto-reviews/Http.cs-r1-20260523.md:10648:./codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r3-20260523.md:3181:tests/Fixtures/api-key-stamper/turnkey-api-key-stamper-vectors.json:11:    "note": "P-256 ECDSA signatures are NOT byte-identical across upstream runtimes when the impl uses random-k (nodecrypto.ts, webcrypto.ts). This C# port mirrors upstream's purejs runtime which uses RFC 6979 deterministic-k AND low-S (noble v1.3.0 default), so the C# signature bytes ARE reproducible against an upstream purejs runtime. All three upstream runtimes produce signatures that verify under the same public key + SHA-256(content), so end-to-end Turnkey API calls work regardless of runtime.",
codex-crypto-reviews/Http.cs-r1-20260523.md:10649:./codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r3-20260523.md:3182:tests/Fixtures/api-key-stamper/turnkey-api-key-stamper-vectors.json:12:    "byte_level_assertion_strategy": "Exact Node-generated byte snapshots would require running the upstream npm package with its full node_modules; the tarball ships only built JS + declarations. Instead, ApiKeyStamperTests asserts wire-format equivalence by: (1) reproducing the upstream stamp-test.ts shape (decoded JSON key order = ['publicKey', 'scheme', 'signature']; scheme constant; publicKey equality); (2) crypto-verifying the DER ECDSA signature against SHA-256(content) under the upstream fixture public key; (3) asserting RFC 6979 determinism (same content -> same signature); (4) asserting low-S (s in (0, n/2]). This combination is sufficient to guarantee the stamp is accepted by Turnkey's backend, which is the wire-format goal."

codex-crypto-reviews/Http.cs-r1-20260523.md:10718:./codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r3-20260523.md:3263:codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r2-20260523.md:398:src/turnkey-sdk-csharp.csproj:12:    <Description>Unofficial / community-maintained Turnkey SDK for .NET by Kyuzan Inc. Not affiliated with Turnkey, Inc. 1:1 logical port of @turnkey/crypto v2.8.8, @turnkey/http v3.16.0, @turnkey/api-key-stamper v0.5.0, and @turnkey/encoding v0.6.0 (peak-pinned versions). Provides P-256 ECDSA / HPKE / HKDF / API key stamping primitives for .NET. BouncyCastle 2.5.0 is used only for primitives; HPKE / HKDF / bundle parsing are direct ports of the upstream Turnkey TypeScript logic.</Description>

codex-crypto-reviews/Http.cs-r1-20260523.md:10845:./codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r3-20260523.md:3465:codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r2-20260523.md:2025:   651	src/turnkey-sdk-csharp.csproj:12:    <Description>Unofficial / community-maintained Turnkey SDK for .NET by Kyuzan Inc. Not affiliated with Turnkey, Inc. 1:1 logical port of @turnkey/crypto v2.8.8, @turnkey/http v3.16.0, @turnkey/api-key-stamper v0.5.0, and @turnkey/encoding v0.6.0 (peak-pinned versions). Provides P-256 ECDSA / HPKE / HKDF / API key stamping primitives for .NET. BouncyCastle 2.5.0 is used only for primitives; HPKE / HKDF / bundle parsing are direct ports of the upstream Turnkey TypeScript logic.</Description>

codex-crypto-reviews/Http.cs-r1-20260523.md:10917:./codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r3-20260523.md:3593:codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r2-20260523.md:2774:  1400	src/turnkey-sdk-csharp.csproj:12:    <Description>Unofficial / community-maintained Turnkey SDK for .NET by Kyuzan Inc. Not affiliated with Turnkey, Inc. 1:1 logical port of @turnkey/crypto v2.8.8, @turnkey/http v3.16.0, @turnkey/api-key-stamper v0.5.0, and @turnkey/encoding v0.6.0 (peak-pinned versions). Provides P-256 ECDSA / HPKE / HKDF / API key stamping primitives for .NET. BouncyCastle 2.5.0 is used only for primitives; HPKE / HKDF / bundle parsing are direct ports of the upstream Turnkey TypeScript logic.</Description>

codex-crypto-reviews/Http.cs-r1-20260523.md:10926:./codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r3-20260523.md:3655:codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r2-20260523.md:2891:  1517	codex-crypto-reviews/Encoding.cs-r1-20260523.md:308:src/turnkey-sdk-csharp.csproj:12:    <Description>Unofficial / community-maintained Turnkey SDK for .NET by Kyuzan Inc. Not affiliated with Turnkey, Inc. 1:1 logical port of @turnkey/crypto v2.8.8, @turnkey/http v3.16.0, @turnkey/api-key-stamper v0.5.0, and @turnkey/encoding v0.6.0 (peak-pinned versions). Provides P-256 ECDSA / HPKE / HKDF / API key stamping primitives for .NET. BouncyCastle 2.5.0 is used only for primitives; HPKE / HKDF / bundle parsing are direct ports of the upstream Turnkey TypeScript logic.</Description>

codex-crypto-reviews/Http.cs-r1-20260523.md:11074:./codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r3-20260523.md:3874:codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r2-20260523.md:4068:  2694	codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r1-20260523.md:651:src/turnkey-sdk-csharp.csproj:12:    <Description>Unofficial / community-maintained Turnkey SDK for .NET by Kyuzan Inc. Not affiliated with Turnkey, Inc. 1:1 logical port of @turnkey/crypto v2.8.8, @turnkey/http v3.16.0, @turnkey/api-key-stamper v0.5.0, and @turnkey/encoding v0.6.0 (peak-pinned versions). Provides P-256 ECDSA / HPKE / HKDF / API key stamping primitives for .NET. BouncyCastle 2.5.0 is used only for primitives; HPKE / HKDF / bundle parsing are direct ports of the upstream Turnkey TypeScript logic.</Description>

codex-crypto-reviews/Http.cs-r1-20260523.md:11159:./codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r3-20260523.md:4087:codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r2-20260523.md:6055:  5535	./src/turnkey-sdk-csharp.csproj:12:    <Description>Unofficial / community-maintained Turnkey SDK for .NET by Kyuzan Inc. Not affiliated with Turnkey, Inc. 1:1 logical port of @turnkey/crypto v2.8.8, @turnkey/http v3.16.0, @turnkey/api-key-stamper v0.5.0, and @turnkey/encoding v0.6.0 (peak-pinned versions). Provides P-256 ECDSA / HPKE / HKDF / API key stamping primitives for .NET. BouncyCastle 2.5.0 is used only for primitives; HPKE / HKDF / bundle parsing are direct ports of the upstream Turnkey TypeScript logic.</Description>

codex-crypto-reviews/Http.cs-r1-20260523.md:11174:./codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r3-20260523.md:4118:codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r2-20260523.md:6604:  6084	- `tests/ApiKeyStamperTests.cs` uses upstream fixture constants inline, but it does not assert exact Node-generated stamp bytes. codex-crypto-reviews/Http.cs-r1-20260523.md:11251:./codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r3-20260523.md:4206:codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r2-20260523.md:7062:   398	src/turnkey-sdk-csharp.csproj:12:    <Description>Unofficial / community-maintained Turnkey SDK for .NET by Kyuzan Inc. Not affiliated with Turnkey, Inc. 1:1 logical port of @turnkey/crypto v2.8.8, @turnkey/http v3.16.0, @turnkey/api-key-stamper v0.5.0, and @turnkey/encoding v0.6.0 (peak-pinned versions). Provides P-256 ECDSA / HPKE / HKDF / API key stamping primitives for .NET. BouncyCastle 2.5.0 is used only for primitives; HPKE / HKDF / bundle parsing are direct ports of the upstream Turnkey TypeScript logic.</Description>

codex-crypto-reviews/Http.cs-r1-20260523.md:11325:./codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r3-20260523.md:4373:codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r2-20260523.md:8658:tests/Fixtures/api-key-stamper/turnkey-api-key-stamper-vectors.json:9:    "note": "P-256 ECDSA signatures are NOT byte-identical across runtimes when the upstream impl uses random-k (nodecrypto.ts, webcrypto.ts). This C# port mirrors upstream's purejs runtime which uses RFC 6979 deterministic-k, so the signature bytes are reproducible. Either way, all signatures verify under the same public key + SHA-256(content)."

codex-crypto-reviews/Http.cs-r1-20260523.md:11417:./codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r3-20260523.md:4888:codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523-prefix-evidence.md:6254:./codex-crypto-reviews/Encoding.cs-r1-20260523.md:308:src/turnkey-sdk-csharp.csproj:12:    <Description>Unofficial / community-maintained Turnkey SDK for .NET by Kyuzan Inc. Not affiliated with Turnkey, Inc. 1:1 logical port of @turnkey/crypto v2.8.8, @turnkey/http v3.16.0, @turnkey/api-key-stamper v0.5.0, and @turnkey/encoding v0.6.0 (peak-pinned versions). Provides P-256 ECDSA / HPKE / HKDF / API key stamping primitives for .NET. BouncyCastle 2.5.0 is used only for primitives; HPKE / HKDF / bundle parsing are direct ports of the upstream Turnkey TypeScript logic.</Description>

codex-crypto-reviews/Http.cs-r1-20260523.md:11626:./codex-crypto-reviews/ApiKeyStamper.cs-r3-20260523.md:6513:codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523.md:14888:./src/turnkey-sdk-csharp.csproj:12:    <Description>Unofficial / community-maintained Turnkey SDK for .NET by Kyuzan Inc. Not affiliated with Turnkey, Inc. 1:1 logical port of @turnkey/crypto v2.8.8, @turnkey/http v3.16.0, @turnkey/api-key-stamper v0.5.0, and @turnkey/encoding v0.6.0 (peak-pinned versions). Provides P-256 ECDSA / HPKE / HKDF / API key stamping primitives for .NET. BouncyCastle 2.5.0 is used only for primitives; HPKE / HKDF / bundle parsing are direct ports of the upstream Turnkey TypeScript logic.</Description>

codex-crypto-reviews/Http.cs-r1-20260523.md:11971:| `FromTargetPrivateKey` `src/Http.cs:92` | README setup pattern `package/README.md:22`, `ApiKeyStamper.constructor` `turnkey-api-key-stamper-0.5.0/ts-source/index.ts:78` | REVIEWED | C# derives public key before constructing stamper. |
codex-crypto-reviews/Http.cs-r1-20260523.md:11977:| `CreateSignedRequest` `src/Http.cs:175` | common stamp pattern `...public_api.client.ts:1631`, `TSignedRequest` `ts-source/base.ts:270` | REVIEWED | Shared C# helper for upstream repeated generated code. |
codex-crypto-reviews/Http.cs-r1-20260523.md:11981:- C# nested DTO classes replace TS generated type aliases; this is structural when JSON key names/order are kept equivalent.
codex-crypto-reviews/Http.cs-r1-20260523.md:11982:- `[JsonPropertyName]` plus `TurnkeyJsonContext` replaces `JSON.stringify`; intended to preserve compact JSON without reflection.
codex-crypto-reviews/Http.cs-r1-20260523.md:11983:- Synchronous C# stamp methods replace `Promise<TSignedRequest>`; stamping is locally synchronous and does not change request bytes.
codex-crypto-reviews/Http.cs-r1-20260523.md:11985:- A shared `CreateSignedRequest` helper replaces repeated generated TS blocks; it emits the same `{body, stamp, url}` object shape.
codex-crypto-reviews/Http.cs-r1-20260523.md:11986:- Selected endpoint methods replace the full generated client surface; structural scope reduction for the peak Unity subset.
codex-crypto-reviews/Http.cs-r1-20260523.md:11997:| `src/Http.cs:226`, `:247`, `:280`, `:304` | `public_api.types.ts:2163`, `:2185`, `:2707`, `:2795` | Submit DTO property order is `organizationId,type,timestampMs,parameters`; upstream generated request declarations are `type,timestampMs,organizationId,parameters,generateAppProofs`. | Use `[JsonPropertyOrder]` or reorder DTO declarations to the chosen upstream canonical order, then fixture it. |
codex-crypto-reviews/Http.cs-r1-20260523.md:11999:| `src/Http.cs:121`, `:136`, `:147`, `:158`, `:169` | `...public_api.client.ts:1630`, `:2900`, `:2963`, `:2995`, `:3089` | C# throws for empty whoami org and null bodies; upstream stamp methods do no such validation before `JSON.stringify`. | Remove for strict parity, or document as intentional validation and test as non-parity. |
codex-crypto-reviews/Http.cs-r1-20260523.md:12003:Not satisfied. `tests/Fixtures/` currently has `encoding`, `crypto`, and `api-key-stamper` fixtures only; there is no `tests/Fixtures/http` fixture exercising `src/Http.cs`. `tests/HttpTests.cs` uses inline assertions and verifies shape/header decodability, but it does not assert byte-for-byte parity against Node output from pinned `@turnkey/http@3.16.0`.
codex-crypto-reviews/Http.cs-r1-20260523.md:12007:- I treated `TurnkeyClient.stamp*` as the upstream reference for `{url, body, stamp}` because the generated `sign*` fetchers are WebAuthn-based.

    "note": "P-256 ECDSA signatures are NOT byte-identical across upstream runtimes when the impl uses random-k (nodecrypto.ts, webcrypto.ts). This C# port mirrors upstream's purejs runtime which uses RFC 6979 deterministic-k AND low-S (noble v1.3.0 default), so the C# signature bytes ARE reproducible against an upstream purejs runtime. All three upstream runtimes produce signatures that verify under the same public key + SHA-256(content), so end-to-end Turnkey API calls work regardless of runtime.",
    "byte_level_assertion_strategy": "Exact Node-generated byte snapshots would require running the upstream npm package with its full node_modules; the tarball ships only built JS + declarations. Instead, ApiKeyStamperTests asserts wire-format equivalence by: (1) reproducing the upstream stamp-test.ts shape (decoded JSON key order = ['publicKey', 'scheme', 'signature']; scheme constant; publicKey equality); (2) crypto-verifying the DER ECDSA signature against SHA-256(content) under the upstream fixture public key; (3) asserting RFC 6979 determinism (same content -> same signature); (4) asserting low-S (s in (0, n/2]). This combination is sufficient to guarantee the stamp is accepted by Turnkey's backend, which is the wire-format goal."
